name: Red Tomato Workspace
description: Workspace layout grouping your emails into high-impact states like Follow-ups, Actions, and Risks.
aiInstructions: Analyze the inbox and organize emails into a work tracker workspace. Extract: lead/client name & subtitle, work type, current state (e.g. Needs action now, Follow-up due, Waiting for reply, Review or decision needed, Attachment review, Missing contact, Deadline risk, Low priority), why it matters, next step, and details/issues.
suggestedPrompts: Organise this inbox around customer success conversations.
Show me all items grouped by client
Show me items that need my reply today

name: Applicant Tracker
description: Track job applicants, their roles, and application status
aiInstructions: Extract candidate/applicant information from each email. Look for job applications, resumes, cover letters, and recruiter correspondence. Group by candidate. Determine status based on email content — new applications are 'New', replies and follow-ups suggest 'Reviewed', mentions of scheduling suggest 'Interview', and rejection language suggests 'Rejected'.
suggestedPrompts: Show me all applicants from this week
Which candidates applied for the engineering role?
Show new applications I haven't reviewed yet

name: Application Tracker
description: Track your own job applications, statuses, and next steps
aiInstructions: Extract job application information from the user's perspective as a job seeker. Look for application confirmations, interview invitations, coding challenges, rejection emails, and offer letters. Determine the status based on the most recent correspondence in each thread.
suggestedPrompts: Which companies have I heard back from?
List applications where I need to take action
Show rejections from the last month

name: Smart Inbox Sorter
description: AI-categorized view of your inbox with priorities and summaries
aiInstructions: Categorize each email by its type and assign a priority level. Generate a brief one-line summary capturing the key information or action required. Categories should reflect the email's purpose (e.g., Newsletter, Notification, Personal, Work, Transaction, Promotion).
suggestedPrompts: Show me high-priority emails from today
What newsletters did I get this week?
